data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The coordinator owning the goroutine provides the serialization guarantee,
37	// so Land() itself does not take any locks.
38	
39	import (
40		"fmt"
41		"os"
42		osexec "os/exec"
43		"path/filepath"
44		"strings"
45	)
46	
47	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
48	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
49	// branch."
50	type LandRequest struct {
51		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
52		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
53		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
54		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
55		WorktreeDir string
56	
57		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
58		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
59		BaseRev string
60	
61		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
62		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
63		ResultRev string
64	
65		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
66		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
67		BeadID string
68	
69		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
70		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
71		// bead do not collide.
72		AttemptID string
73	
74		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
75		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
76		TargetBranch string
77	
78		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
79		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
80		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
81		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
82		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
83		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
84		EvidenceDir string
85	}
86	
87	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
88	type LandResult struct {
89		// Status is one of:
90		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
91		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
92		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
93		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
94		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
95		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
96		Status string
97	
98		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
99		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
100		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
101		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
102		NewTip string
103	
104		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
105		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
106		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
107		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
108		// unchanged.
109		Merged bool
110	
111		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
112		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
113		PreserveRef string
114	
115		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
116		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
117		Reason string
118	
119		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
120		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
121		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
122		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
123		PushFailed bool
124	
125		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
126		PushError string
127	
128		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
129		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
130		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
131		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
132		MergedCommitCount int
133	
134		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
135		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
136		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
137		EvidenceCommitSHA string
138	}
139	
140	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
141	type PreClaimResult struct {
142		// Action is one of:
143		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
144		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
145		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
146		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
147		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
148		Action    string
149		LocalSHA  string
150		OriginSHA string
151	}
152	
153	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
154	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
155	type LandingGitOps interface {
156		// HasRemote reports whether the given remote name exists in dir.
157		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool
158	
159		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
160		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
161		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
162	
163		// FetchBranch fetches remote/branch into dir (no merge, no checkout).
164		// Returns nil when no remote exists.
165		FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error
166	
167		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
168		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
169		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
170	
171		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
172		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
173		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
174	
175		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
176		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
177		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
178		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
179		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
180		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
181		//
182		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
183		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
184		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
185		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
186		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
187		// would see stale content.
188		//
189		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
190		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
191		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
192		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
193	
194		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
195		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
196	
197		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
198		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
199	
200		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
201		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
202		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
203		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
204		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
205		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
206		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
207	
208		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
209		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
210	
211		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
212		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
213		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
214		PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error
215	
216		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
217		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
218		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
219		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
220		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
221		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
222		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
223	
224		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
225		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
226		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
227		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
228	
229		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
230		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
231	
232		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
233		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
234		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
235		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
236	}
237	
238	// RealLandingGitOps implements LandingGitOps via os/exec git commands.
239	type RealLandingGitOps struct{}
240	
241	func (RealLandingGitOps) HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool {
242		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "remote").Output()
243		if err != nil {
244			return false
245		}
246		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
247			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == remote {
248				return true
249			}
250		}
251		return false
252	}
253	
254	func (RealLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
255		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "symbolic-ref", "--short", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
256		if err != nil {
257			return "", fmt.Errorf("git symbolic-ref HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
258		}
259		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
260	}
261	
262	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error {
263		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "fetch", remote, branch).CombinedOutput()
264		if err != nil {
265			return fmt.Errorf("git fetch %s %s: %s: %w", remote, branch, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
266		}
267		return nil
268	}
269	
270	func (RealLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
271		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).CombinedOutput()
272		if err != nil {
273			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
274		}
275		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
276	}
277	
278	func (RealLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
279		args := []string{"-C", dir, "update-ref", ref, sha}
280		if oldSHA != "" {
281			args = append(args, oldSHA)
282		}
283		out, err := osexec.Command("git", args...).CombinedOutput()
284		if err != nil {
285			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
286		}
287		return nil
288	}
289	
290	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
291		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
292		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
293		// calls from building stale trees.
294		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
295		if err != nil {
296			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
297		}
298	
299		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
300		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
301		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
302		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
303		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
304		if fromRev == "" {
305			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
306			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
307			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
308			return nil
309		}
310		diffOut, diffErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
311		if diffErr != nil {
312			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
313			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
314			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
315			return nil
316		}
317		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
318		if len(changed) == 0 {
319			return nil
320		}
321	
322		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
323		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
324		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
325		// those ourselves.
326		var indexFiles []string
327		var removedFiles []string
328		for _, f := range changed {
329			probe := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
330			if probe.Run() == nil {
331				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
332			} else {
333				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
334			}
335		}
336	
337		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
338		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
339		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
340		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
341			args := []string{"-C", dir, "checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
342			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
343			out2, err2 := osexec.Command("git", args...).CombinedOutput()
344			if err2 != nil {
345				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
346			}
347		}
348	
349		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
350		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
351		// working-tree update.
352		for _, f := range removedFiles {
353			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
354			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
355		}
356	
357		return nil
358	}
359	
360	func (RealLandingGitOps) AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error {
361		// --detach so the worktree does not create a persistent branch.
362		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "worktree", "add", "--force", "--detach", path, rev).CombinedOutput()
363		if err != nil {
364			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add %s %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
365		}
366		return nil
367	}
368	
369	func (RealLandingGitOps) RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error {
370		_ = osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", path).Run()
371		_ = osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
372		return nil
373	}
374	
375	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
376		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
377		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
378		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
379		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
380		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
381		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
382		out, err := osexec.Command(
383			"git", "-C", wtDir,
384			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
385			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
386			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
387		).CombinedOutput()
388		if err != nil {
389			_ = osexec.Command("git", "-C", wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
390			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
391		}
392		return nil
393	}
394	
395	func (RealLandingGitOps) HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error) {
396		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
397		if err != nil {
398			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
399		}
400		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
401	}
402	
403	func (RealLandingGitOps) PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error {
404		// Refspec "<local>:<remote>" with no '+' prefix → fast-forward only.
405		refspec := fmt.Sprintf("%s:refs/heads/%s", localRef, targetBranch)
406		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "push", remote, refspec).CombinedOutput()
407		if err != nil {
408			return fmt.Errorf("git push %s %s: %s: %w", remote, refspec, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
409		}
410		return nil
411	}
412	
413	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
414		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
415		if err != nil {
416			return 0
417		}
418		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
419		n := 0
420		for _, c := range s {
421			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
422				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')
423			}
424		}
425		return n
426	}
427	
428	func (RealLandingGitOps) StageDir(dir, relPath string) error {
429		// Exclude embedded session logs from the evidence commit — tracking
430		// multi-thousand-line .jsonl files caused retry review prompts to
431		// balloon past 2M tokens and crash every provider with n_keep > n_ctx
432		// (ddx-39e27896). manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, and
433		// usage.json remain tracked for audit; the raw session log lives on
434		// disk, not in git history.
435		args := append([]string{"-C", dir, "add", "--", relPath}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
436		out, err := osexec.Command("git", args...).CombinedOutput()
437		if err != nil {
438			return fmt.Errorf("git add %s: %s: %w", relPath, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
439		}
440		return nil
441	}
442	
443	func (RealLandingGitOps) CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error) {
444		out, _ := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
445		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
446			return "", nil
447		}
448		commitOut, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir,
449			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
450			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
451			"commit", "-m", msg,
452		).CombinedOutput()
453		if err != nil {
454			return "", fmt.Errorf("git commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
455		}
456		shaOut, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
457		if err != nil {
458			return "", fmt.Errorf("rev-parse HEAD after evidence commit: %w", err)
459		}
460		return strings.TrimSpace(string(shaOut)), nil
461	}
462	
463	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck implements LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck.
464	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
465		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
466		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
467		if err != nil {
468			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
469			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
470		}
471		_ = out // remote URL not needed
472	
473		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
474		if fetchOut, fetchErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
475			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)), fetchErr)
476		}
477	
478		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
479		localOut, localErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
480		if localErr != nil {
481			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
482		}
483		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
484	
485		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
486		originOut, originErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
487		if originErr != nil {
488			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
489		}
490		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
491	
492		// Step 5: compare.
493		if localSHA == originSHA {
494			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
495		}
496	
497		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
498		localAncestorErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
499		if localAncestorErr == nil {
500			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
501			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
502			if upErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
503				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
504			}
505			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
506			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
507			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
508			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
509			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
510			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
511		}
512	
513		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
514		originAncestorErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
515		if originAncestorErr == nil {
516			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
517		}
518	
519		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
520		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
521	}
522	
523	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
524	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
525	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
526	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
527	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
528		short := tip
529		if len(short) > 12 {
530			short = short[:12]
531		}
532		attempt := attemptID
533		if attempt == "" {
534			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
535		}
536		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
537	}
538	
539	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
540	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
541	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
542	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
543	//
544	// Best-effort: only works when HEAD is a symbolic ref to targetBranch (the
545	// normal case for server workers and CLI users). When HEAD is detached or
546	// points elsewhere, the evidence commit is silently skipped.
547	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
548		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
549		if err != nil || branch != targetBranch {
550			return
551		}
552		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
553			return
554		}
555		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
556		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
557		if err != nil || sha == "" {
558			return
559		}
560		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
561		result.NewTip = sha
562	}
563	
564	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
565	// Callers MUST serialize calls per projectRoot (the server coordinator
566	// goroutine provides this). Land() itself takes no internal locks.
567	//
568	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
569	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
570	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
571	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
572	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
573		if gitOps == nil {
574			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
575		}
576		wd := req.WorktreeDir
577		if wd == "" {
578			wd = projectRoot
579		}
580	
581		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
582		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
583			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
584		}
585	
586		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
587		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
588		if targetBranch == "" {
589			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
590			if err != nil {
591				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
592			}
593			targetBranch = br
594		}
595		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
596	
597		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
598		// read the local branch directly.
599		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
600		if hasOrigin {
601			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
602			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
603			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
604			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
605		}
606		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
607		if err != nil {
608			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
609		}
610	
611		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
612	
613		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
614		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
615		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
616		// saw. No merge commit is created.
617		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
618			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
619				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
620			}
621			// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files so the
622			// operator and subsequent agents see the newly-landed commit's
623			// changes. Pass currentTip as fromRev so only files actually
624			// changed by this commit are restored — unrelated local
625			// modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved.
626			// Non-fatal on error.
627			_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
628			result := &LandResult{
629				Status:            "landed",
630				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
631				Merged:            false,
632				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
633			}
634			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
635				landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
636			}
637			landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
638			return result, nil
639		}
640	
641		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
642		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
643		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
644		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
645		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
646		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
647		if tempWtErr != nil {
648			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
649		}
650		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
651		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
652		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
653		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
654			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
655		}
656		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
657	
658		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
659		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
660			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
661			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
662			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
663				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
664			}
665			return &LandResult{
666				Status:            "preserved",
667				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
668				Reason:            "merge conflict",
669				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
670			}, nil
671		}
672	
673		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
674		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
675		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
676		if err != nil {
677			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
678		}
679		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
680			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
681		}
682		// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files. Pass
683		// currentTip as fromRev; the merge commit's tree contains both the
684		// current-tip files and the incoming ResultRev files, so the diff
685		// against currentTip yields exactly the files affected by the merge.
686		// Unrelated local modifications are preserved.
687		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
688	
689		result := &LandResult{
690			Status:            "landed",
691			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
692			Merged:            true,
693			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
694		}
695		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
696			landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
697		}
698		landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
699		return result, nil
700	}
701	
702	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
703	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
704	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
705	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
706		if !hasOrigin {
707			return
708		}
709		if err := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch); err != nil {
710			result.PushFailed = true
711			result.PushError = err.Error()
712		}
713	}
714	
715	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
716	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
717	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
718		if attemptID != "" {
719			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
720				return attemptID[:16]
721			}
722			return attemptID
723		}
724		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
725	}
726	
727	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
728	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
729	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
730	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
731		if land == nil || res == nil {
732			return
733		}
734		switch land.Status {
735		case "landed":
736			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
737			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
738			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
739			res.Outcome = "merged"
740			res.Reason = ""
741			res.PreserveRef = ""
742			if land.Merged {
743				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
744			}
745			if land.PushFailed {
746				res.Reason = "landed locally; push failed: " + land.PushError
747			}
748			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
749			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
750			if land.NewTip != "" {
751				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
752			}
753		case "preserved":
754			res.Outcome = "preserved"
755			res.Reason = land.Reason
756			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
757		case "no-changes":
758			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
759			// a richer no-changes rationale.
760			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
761				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
762			}
763			if res.Reason == "" {
764				res.Reason = land.Reason
765			}
766		}
767		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
768		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
769		if land.Status == "preserved" {
770			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
771			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
772			reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
773		}
774		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
775		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
776	}
777	
778	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
779	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
780	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
781	// time Land() runs.
782	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
783		return LandRequest{
784			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
785			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
786			ResultRev:    res.ResultRev,
787			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
788			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
789			TargetBranch: "",
790			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
791		}
792	}

1	package git
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// Command returns a prepared *exec.Cmd for a git invocation that honors
13	// cmd.Dir / -C <path> rather than whatever GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc.
14	// were set in the parent environment.
15	//
16	// Why this wrapper exists (ddx-aa8a5fb3):
17	// Git's repository-discovery rules mean that if GIT_DIR is set in the
18	// process environment, git uses THAT repo and ignores both -C <path> and
19	// the process's working directory for repository selection. ddx commands
20	// invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit, pre-push, etc.) inherit
21	// the hook's GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE. Without stripping
22	// those, any exec.Command("git", ...) inside ddx mutates the hook's
23	// repository (the outer one), producing core.bare=true flips and stray
24	// core.worktree entries in .git/config.
25	//
26	// The wrapper strips exactly git's "local env vars" set (the vars git
27	// itself clears when spawning worktree subprocesses). This is the
28	// canonical set documented by `git rev-parse --local-env-vars`. It
29	// preserves everything else, including GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_TRACE*,
30	// GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL, and custom debugging overrides.
31	//
32	// Dir must be an absolute or working-directory-relative path; it is set
33	// on the returned cmd so that when no GIT_* local vars are in the
34	// environment, git discovers the repo from Dir.
35	func Command(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
36		warnOnce.Do(maybeWarnGitDirLeaked)
37	
38		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
39		if dir != "" {
40			cmd.Dir = dir
41		}
42		cmd.Env = CleanEnv()
43		return cmd
44	}
45	
46	// CleanEnv returns os.Environ() with every git local-env-var removed.
47	// Exported so sibling packages (e.g. internal/agent) that spawn NON-git
48	// subprocesses inside a worktree can use the same scrub — those child
49	// processes often invoke git themselves and need the same protection.
50	func CleanEnv() []string {
51		vars := localEnvVars()
52		src := os.Environ()
53		out := make([]string, 0, len(src))
54		for _, kv := range src {
55			eq := strings.IndexByte(kv, '=')
56			if eq < 0 {
57				out = append(out, kv)
58				continue
59			}
60			if vars[kv[:eq]] {
61				continue
62			}
63			out = append(out, kv)
64		}
65		return out
66	}
67	
68	// localEnvVars returns the set of environment variable names that git
69	// treats as repository-local (i.e. scoped to the current git process's
70	// repository selection and cleared when git itself spawns subprocesses
71	// for other worktrees). Result is cached for process lifetime.
72	//
73	// We query `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` once at first use so we pick
74	// up any vars a newer git version adds. If that query fails (git not on
75	// PATH, or restricted environment), fall back to the documented set.
76	func localEnvVars() map[string]bool {
77		localEnvVarsOnce.Do(func() {
78			set := map[string]bool{}
79			// Use exec.Command directly here to avoid recursion through
80			// Command(). This runs with inherited env on purpose: even if
81			// GIT_DIR is set, `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` is a static
82			// list of variable NAMES (not their values), so inherited env
83			// doesn't affect the output.
84			out, err := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars").Output()
85			if err == nil {
86				for _, name := range strings.Fields(string(out)) {
87					if name != "" {
88						set[name] = true
89					}
90				}
91			}
92			// Fallback / supplement: the canonical set from git's source
93			// (environment.c local_repo_env[]). Kept inline so ddx remains
94			// functional even if `git rev-parse` is unavailable.
95			for _, name := range []string{
96				"GIT_DIR",
97				"GIT_WORK_TREE",
98				"GIT_INDEX_FILE",
99				"GIT_COMMON_DIR",
100				"GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY",
101				"GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES",
102				"GIT_PREFIX",
103				"GIT_INTERNAL_GETTEXT_SH_SCHEME",
104				"GIT_SHALLOW_FILE",
105				"GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS",
106				"GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE",
107				"GIT_CONFIG",
108				"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL",
109				"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM",
110				"GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS",
111				"GIT_CONFIG_COUNT",
112				"GIT_INDEX_VERSION",
113				"GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS",
114				"GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS",
115				"GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS",
116				"GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS",
117				"GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH",
118				"GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK",
119				"GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE",
120			} {
121				set[name] = true
122			}
123			localEnvVarsCache = set
124		})
125		return localEnvVarsCache
126	}
127	
128	var (
129		localEnvVarsOnce  sync.Once
130		localEnvVarsCache map[string]bool
131	
132		warnOnce sync.Once
133	)
134	
135	// maybeWarnGitDirLeaked emits a one-time stderr diagnostic when the
136	// parent environment has GIT_DIR set. This is the canonical smoke signal
137	// for hook-inherited env contamination; surfacing it during normal runs
138	// helps catch upstream leaks we haven't yet wrapped.
139	func maybeWarnGitDirLeaked() {
140		if v := os.Getenv("GIT_DIR"); v != "" {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
142				"ddx git wrapper: stripped inherited GIT_DIR=%q (likely hook context). "+
143					"This is diagnostic; operation proceeds against cmd.Dir instead.\n", v)
144		}
145	}
